properties.summary: Mixed grazing and remnant woodland parcel used as a prototype stand-in for registry-derived boundaries.
properties.carbon_detail: Moderate-to-strong soil organic carbon potential based on public national soil-carbon mapping.
properties.soil_detail: Stable upland soils with moderate erosion sensitivity indicated in public soil grid products.
properties.water_detail: Seasonal drainage influence and limited riparian reach in nearby public hydrology layers.
properties.biodiversity_detail: Remnant woodland intersects public biodiversity significance grids at a moderate level.

properties.summary: Escarpment-edge parcel with native vegetation and strong biodiversity relevance in the prototype.
properties.carbon_detail: Carbon opportunity is moderate; terrain and existing woody cover suggest sequestration potential.
properties.soil_detail: Steeper terrain lowers soil resilience and raises restoration complexity.
properties.water_detail: Less direct waterway exposure than other parcels in this prototype catchment.
properties.biodiversity_detail: Strong overlap with publicly available species and vegetation significance layers.

properties.summary: Alluvial flats parcel with stronger water and soil sensitivity scores in the prototype.
properties.carbon_detail: Alluvial soils show moderate carbon opportunity in public soil-organic-carbon datasets.
properties.soil_detail: Fertile flats with stronger soil function but some compaction risk.
properties.water_detail: High riparian relevance due to creek adjacency and catchment proximity.
properties.biodiversity_detail: Biodiversity value is present but more fragmented than the escarpment parcel.
